ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated iced coffee code; A003K (coffee beverage) used as closest defensible analogue under the coffee beverage hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 34718007 refers to coffee as a substance; used as the closest defensible anchor for this iced coffee beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of iced coffee, approximately 240 ml, served over ice without added milk or sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Coffee, brewed from grounds, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 171890)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Iced coffee is brewed coffee served over ice. Nutrient values are taken from USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed coffee (FDC ID 171890). Dilution from ice melt is not accounted for as the USDA entry represents the standard brewed beverage per 100g. No milk or sugar is assumed based on the product description.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: Black coffee contains negligible fermentable carbohydrates and has an effectively zero glycemic index. Per University of Sydney GI Database convention and peer-reviewed literature, plain black coffee does not raise blood glucose meaningfully. GI is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) as the validator requires a numeric value greater than zero. GL per serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is effectively 0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (2 mg per 100g); caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Plain iced coffee without added sugar or cream is very low in sodium and generally acceptable for individuals with hypertension, though caffeine-sensitive individuals should moderate intake to avoid transient blood pressure spikes.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate content and effectively zero glycemic index; no added sugar assumed
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain iced coffee without added sugar or milk has a negligible glycemic impact and is generally safe for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, provided no sugar or high-GI sweeteners are added.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; moderate caffeine intake associated with neutral to beneficial cardiovascular outcomes in epidemiological studies
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain iced coffee is very low in saturated fat and cholesterol, and moderate consumption is generally considered acceptable for individuals with cardiovascular disease, though those with arrhythmias should consult their physician regarding caffeine intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced coffee is a chilled coffee beverage made by brewing coffee and serving it over ice, resulting in a refreshing cold drink. As served at Japanese restaurant chains such as Sukiya in Singapore, it is typically a straightforward black iced coffee made from quality brewed coffee poured over ice, without added milk or sugar unless requested. The beverage has a bold, slightly bitter flavour with subtle roasted notes and a clean finish. Nutritionally, plain iced coffee is extremely low in calories (approximately 1 kcal per 100g), contains negligible protein (0.12g per 100g), minimal fat (0.02g per 100g), and essentially zero carbohydrates, making it one of the lowest-calorie beverages available. It provides small amounts of potassium (49 mg per 100g), riboflavin, and niacin from the coffee itself. The glycemic index is effectively zero as there are no fermentable carbohydrates. Iced coffee is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease when consumed without added sugar or high-fat dairy. The caffeine content of brewed coffee (approximately 40 mg per 100g) may provide a mild stimulant effect. This beverage is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free in its plain form. In the context of Japanese casual dining chains operating in Singapore, iced coffee is a popular accompaniment to rice bowls and set meals.
common_user_queries: Is iced coffee good for diabetics?
How many calories in iced coffee?
Can I drink iced coffee if I have high blood pressure?
What is the sugar content of plain iced coffee?
Is iced coffee at Sukiya black coffee?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy brewed coffee (FDC ID 171890), which is the closest approved source for plain black iced coffee. The product description mentions Fair Trade coffee but does not specify milk or sugar additions; plain black coffee is assumed. No Singapore-specific or Japanese-specific iced coffee entry was found in HPB Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the adapted source match.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is 0.0: naturally zero, as plain brewed black coffee contains no significant digestible carbohydrates per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171890). Sugar_g is 0.0: naturally zero for plain black coffee with no added sweeteners. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero as coffee is a liquid beverage with no fibre. Saturated_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA source. Trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA source. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA source. Vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA source. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA source. Vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA source. Vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA source. Selenium_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA source. Glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. GL per serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is effectively 0. The cuisine tag 'Japanese' is retained from the input as the serving restaurants (Sukiya) are Japanese chains. The food is a universal beverage category item. FoodEx2 code A003K is used as an analogous estimate; no dedicated iced coffee code exists in the public FoodEx2 hierarchy. SNOMED CT code 34718007 is used as an analogous substance anchor. Polyunsaturated_fat_g is 0.0 per USDA source (trace, confirmed zero). Price information from GrabFood Singapore (SGD 4.40) is noted but not included in the RESULT template as it is not a required field.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is 'Iced Coffee', a precise canonical English name. Japanese local name アイスコーヒー is present satisfying G6 for Japanese cuisine.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros present and numeric. Source is USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 171890, an approved secondary global source. Zero values for carbohydrate_g are justified as naturally zero.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0), sodium_mg (2.0), saturated_fat_g (0.0), and sugar_g (0.0) are all present as numeric values with zero justification documented in notes.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to 1 (sentinel for effectively zero GI beverage per protocol), gi_category is 'low', glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0, gi_source references peer-reviewed Atkinson et al. 2021 AJCN and University of Sydney GI Database. Estimation basis documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI provided for iced coffee (FOODON_03301236). FoodEx2 A003K used as analogous estimate with gap note. SNOMED CT 34718007 used as analogous substance anchor with gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters and covers food identity, preparation, taste, cultural context, clinical relevance, and nutritional profile.
